paper.title: Milky Way archaeology using RR Lyrae and type II Cepheids II. High velocity RR Lyrae stars, and mass of the Milky Way

extraction.summary: Extracted 9 high-velocity RR Lyrae candidates with P_unb > 0.5 from Prudil et al. (2022). All candidates are explicitly presented as likely unbound. HV-RRL-03 and HV-RRL-04 have uncertain RR Lyrae classifications per the paper. HV-RRL-02 and HV-RRL-08 show tentative orbital association with the Sagittarius dwarf spheroidal galaxy.

method_chain.summary: Input catalogs: Gaia EDR3 astrometry and photometry, PanSTARRS-1 i-band photometry, SDSS DR15 spectra, LAMOST DR6 spectra, and CSS variable-star photometry.

source_refs.context: Dataset description drawing from Gaia EDR3, PanSTARRS-1, SDSS DR15, and LAMOST DR6

method_chain.summary: Cross-matched the RR Lyrae catalog (Gaia DR2 + CSS) with LAMOST DR6 spectra within 1 arcsec, yielding 3003 matches.

source_refs.context: Cross-match of RR Lyrae catalog with LAMOST DR6

method_chain.summary: Astrometric quality cuts on proper-motion significance, ipd_gof_harmonic_amplitude, ipd_frac_multi_peak, astrometric_excess_noise_sig, visibility_periods_used, and RUWE scaling. Reduced sample from 6187 to 4847.
outputs: study sample of 4847 RR Lyrae stars

source_refs.context: Astrometric cuts and quality metrics

method_chain.summary: Heliocentric distances estimated via the period-luminosity-metallicity (PLZ) relation using PanSTARRS-1 i-band mean magnitudes, metallicities from Crestani2021, and Schlafly2011 extinction maps.

source_refs.context: Distance estimation via PLZ relation using PanSTARRS-1 i-band

method_chain.summary: Systemic radial velocities measured from SDSS and LAMOST low-resolution spectra using iSpec cross-correlation of Balmer lines and photometric phase templates.

source_refs.context: Radial velocity measurement via iSpec cross-correlation and systemic velocity templates

method_chain.summary: Pulsation periods and amplitudes derived from Catalina Sky Survey photometry using period analysis.

source_refs.context: Table of pulsation and chemical properties from CSS photometry

method_chain.summary: Metallicities [Fe/H] derived using the delta-S method from low-resolution spectra.

source_refs.context: Metallicity derived using the delta-S method

method_chain.summary: Galactocentric coordinates and velocities calculated using galpy with Solar parameters R_sun=8.2 kpc, Z_sun=20.8 pc, solar peculiar motion (11.1,12.24,7.25) km/s, and rotation velocity 230 km/s. Full covariance propagated via 100k MC iterations.

source_refs.context: Galactocentric velocity calculation setup

method_chain.summary: MWPotential2014 composite potential (Miyamoto-Nagai disk, power-law bulge, NFW halo) and two variants with 25% and 50% higher mass.

source_refs.context: MWPotential2014 description and variants

method_chain.summary: Unbound probability P_unb estimated by Monte Carlo sampling: fraction of 100k iterations where v_GC exceeds local v_esc from MWPotential2014. Unbound criterion: P_unb > 0.5.

source_refs.context: Escape velocity and unbound probability definition

method_chain.summary: Backward orbit integration for 6 Gyr to identify disk/bulge crossings and for 3 Gyr to test Sagittarius association.

source_refs.context: Orbit integration and minimum crossing radius analysis

method_chain.summary: Tentative association with Sagittarius dSph based on orbital proximity over 3 Gyr using MC error propagation.

source_refs.context: Sagittarius association assessment

method_chain.summary: RR Lyrae sub-type classification (RRab vs RRc) based on CSS photometric period analysis.

source_refs.context: RR Lyrae sub-class from CSS photometry

method_chain.summary: Direct adoption of Gaia EDR3 catalog photometry values (G magnitude and BP-RP color) without additional processing.

source_refs.context: Gaia photometry from catalog

inclusion_assessment.summary: HV-RRL-01 is presented as a likely unbound RR Lyrae candidate with P_unb = 0.95. The paper notes that candidates at large distances may be 1-2 sigma outliers due to proper-motion uncertainties.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Candidate is clearly listed in the unbound candidate tables with P_unb > 0.5 and explicitly discussed as a high-velocity RR Lyrae star.

source_refs.context: Abstract: identified nine RR Lyrae stars with Galactocentric velocities exceeding the local escape velocity
Section 3: found in total nine RR Lyrae variables that fulfilled the unbound condition
Abstract reports discovery of high-velocity candidates among RR Lyrae stars
Table of unbound RR Lyrae candidates with astrometric properties from Gaia catalog

unbound_probability.description: Probability that the star exceeds the local escape velocity in the assumed MW potential

other.name: pulsation_amplitude_v_css
other.description: Amplitude of total light variations in the V-band from Catalina Sky Survey photometry

quality_flags.name: RUWE
quality_flags.description: Renormalized unit weight error from Gaia EDR3 astrometric solution

quality_flags.name: duplicated_source
quality_flags.description: None of the candidates were flagged as duplicated_source in Gaia EDR3

source_refs.context: None of the candidates had been flagged as duplicated_source in Gaia EDR3

extra.name: rr_lyrae_type
extra.description: RR Lyrae sub-type classification (RRab = fundamental mode, RRc = first overtone)

inclusion_assessment.summary: HV-RRL-03 is presented as a likely unbound RR Lyrae candidate with P_unb = 1.00, but the paper notes its classification as an RR Lyrae star is highly uncertain (HV-RRL-03) or incorrect (HV-RRL-04).
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Paper expresses strong doubt about the RR Lyrae classification of this candidate, suggesting it is probably bound due to misclassification, though it is still listed in the unbound candidate tables.

candidate_groups_considered.description: Nine RR Lyrae stars with P_unb > 0.5 identified as likely unbound high-velocity candidates.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: The paper explicitly presents these objects as possibly unbound from the Milky Way based on Galactocentric velocities exceeding the local escape velocity.

source_refs.context: Abstract identifies nine RR Lyrae stars exceeding local escape velocity
Table of likely unbound RR Lyrae variables with P_unb > 0.5

candidate_groups_considered.description: RR Lyrae variables with v_GC > 250 km/s that remain bound to the Milky Way, used for escape velocity modeling.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: The paper explicitly states these objects remain bound to the Milky Way and uses them only for modeling the high-velocity tail, not as unbound candidates.

source_refs.context: Section focuses on RR Lyrae variables that possess high v_GC but remain bound to the MW
